skill.name: bats-testing-patterns
skill.description: Master Bash Automated Testing System (Bats) for comprehensive shell script testing. Use when writing tests for shell scripts, CI/CD pipelines, or requiring test-driven development of shell utilities.
skill.summary: Comprehensive guidance for writing unit tests for shell scripts using Bats, including test patterns, fixtures, and best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides documentation and examples for writing BATS tests. All 91 static findings are false positives: external commands are code examples in markdown, network URLs are documentation links, filesystem patterns are legitimate test setup patterns, and weak crypto detection is a false positive due to 'Bats' being misidentified as a cryptographic reference.

content.user_title: Write Robust Shell Script Tests with BATS
content.value_statement: Learn comprehensive BATS testing patterns to write maintainable, reliable tests for shell scripts in CI/CD pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Write unit tests for shell scripts using BATS framework
Create test fixtures and helper libraries for complex test scenarios
Implement setup and teardown patterns for test isolation
Mock and stub external commands and functions for unit testing
Test error conditions and edge cases in shell scripts
Integrate BATS tests into CI/CD workflows
limitations: Only tests shell script behavior, not other programming languages
Does not provide integration testing beyond shell execution
Requires BATS to be installed on the test environment
Shell compatibility varies across different shells (bash, sh, dash)

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Testing Infrastructure Scripts
use_cases.description: Write automated tests for deployment scripts, configuration management scripts, and infrastructure automation to ensure reliability.

use_cases.title: Developer Adding Tests to Shell Utilities
use_cases.description: Implement test-driven development for CLI tools and shell utilities to catch regressions early.

use_cases.title: Team Establishing Shell Script Quality Standards
use_cases.description: Set up comprehensive testing patterns and best practices across a team to maintain consistent code quality.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Test Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a BATS test file for my shell script at script.sh that tests the exit code and output of the main function.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to add your first test to a shell script

prompt_templates.title: Test with Fixtures
prompt_templates.prompt: Write BATS tests that use fixture files from tests/fixtures/ directory to test file processing functions.
prompt_templates.scenario: When testing functions that read or write files

prompt_templates.title: Mocking External Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to stub the 'curl' command in BATS to test an API function without making real network requests.
prompt_templates.scenario: When testing scripts that depend on external commands

prompt_templates.title: CI/CD Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow and Makefile to run BATS tests in CI/CD with TAP output.
prompt_templates.scenario: When setting up automated test execution

output_examples.input: Write tests for a script that processes CSV files
output: A BATS test file with tests for file existence, content validation, error handling for malformed CSV, and success cases

output_examples.input: How do I test that my script fails correctly with invalid input?
output: Example showing @test blocks for missing arguments, invalid options, permission denied scenarios, and assertion of error messages

output_examples.input: Set up testing for a CLI tool with multiple commands
output: Test structure with helper functions, fixture setup, teardown cleanup, and tests for help output, version flag, and main command execution

best_practices: Use descriptive test names that clearly state what is being tested and expected behavior
Always clean up temporary files and resources in teardown functions to prevent test pollution
Mock external dependencies like API calls and file system operations to isolate unit tests
anti_patterns: Testing multiple unrelated assertions in a single test function violates single responsibility
Skipping teardown or cleanup leads to flaky tests and resource leaks
Testing implementation details rather than observable behavior makes tests brittle

faq.question: What is BATS?
faq.answer: BATS (Bash Automated Testing System) is a testing framework for shell scripts that provides TAP-compliant output and simple test syntax.

faq.question: How do I install BATS?
faq.answer: Install via Homebrew (brew install bats-core), npm (npm install --global bats), or clone from GitHub and run the install script.

faq.question: What is the difference between setup and setup_file?
faq.answer: setup() runs before each test, while setup_file() runs once before all tests in a file. Use setup_file for expensive shared resources.

faq.question: How do I test that a command fails?
faq.answer: Use 'run' to execute the command, then assert that $status is non-zero and optionally check the error output.

faq.question: Can I test scripts that use external commands?
faq.answer: Yes, use function mocking with 'export -f' or command stubbing by creating mock executables in a temporary PATH directory.

faq.question: How do I run tests in parallel?
faq.answer: Use the --parallel flag with bats, for example: bats tests/*.bats --parallel 4
